title: Can't get the value that would be assigned in the next GEN-FOR iteration.
body:
Hi,
I‘m using the release version of verilator-4.202 and here is my testcase:
```verilog
// t.v
module t (
    input   logic   clk
);

logic [7:0]    mx;
logic [7:0]    A[3];
logic [7:0]    B[3];

always @ (posedge clk) begin
    mx <= mx + 1'b1;
    $write("\n -- %d\n", mx);
    $write("A:   %d    %d    %d\n", A[0], A[1], A[2]);
    $write("B:   %d    %d    %d\n", B[0], B[1], B[2]);

    if (mx == 'd5) begin
        $finish;
    end
end

genvar i;
generate
    for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) begin
        if (i == 2) begin
            always @ (*) begin
                A[i] = mx;
                B[i] = mx;
            end
        end
        else begin
            always @ (*) begin
                A[i] = mx;
                B[i] = A[i+1]; // Use the value that would be assigned in the next GEN-FOR iteration.
            end
        end
    end
endgenerate

endmodule

```
And the output is:
```txt
 --   0
A:     0      0      0
B:     0      0      0

 --   1
A:     1      1      1
B:     0      1      1

 --   2
A:     2      2      2
B:     1      2      2

 --   3
A:     3      3      3
B:     2      3      3

 --   4
A:     4      4      4
B:     3      4      4

 --   5
A:     5      5      5
B:     4      5      5
```
In my RTL design, `B[0]` should be equal to `A[1]`, but it’s not the case.

Post part of the output cpp file here:
```cpp
// ...
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__mx = __Vdly__t__DOT__mx;
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__A[2U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__mx;
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__B[2U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__mx;
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__A[0U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__mx;
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__B[0U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__A[1U]; // B[0] is assigned by A[1]
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__A[1U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__mx; // A[1] is ready.
    vlTOPp->t__DOT__B[1U] = vlTOPp->t__DOT__A[2U];
// ...
```
As you can see, the `B[0]` is assigned by `A[1]`  before `A[1]` is ready. This must be wrong.

I guess this is a bug of Verilator, what's your opinion.
